description:
You are the system administrator of a small web-hosting service.  
A nightly job places a raw log of **usernames**, one per line, in

/home/user/data/users.log

Your task:

1. Read the existing /home/user/data/users.log file.  
2. Produce a frequency report that counts how many times each distinct username appears in the log.  
3. Write the report to a new file named

/home/user/data/user_frequency.txt

4. Formatting rules that the automated checker will validate:

   • Each output line must contain exactly two fields separated by a single space:  
     username␠count

   • The list must be sorted in two passes:  
     a) primary key: descending numerical count (highest first)  
     b) secondary key: ascending lexicographic order of the username for ties

   • No leading or trailing spaces, no blank lines, no header line.

   • The file must end with a single newline character.

5. Do not delete or modify the original users.log file.

Hints for yourself (not commands): you’ll likely want a pipeline that combines sort, uniq, awk/cut or similar tools; remember to convert «uniq -c» output into the required “username␠count” order and then resort by the rules above.

When you are finished, you should be able to:

cat /home/user/data/user_frequency.txt

and see the report in the exact layout described.
truth:
Initial log file already present before the agent starts:

Path: /home/user/data/users.log  
Contents (each entry on its own line, final newline at EOF):

alice
bob
charlie
alice
bob
alice

bob

trent

trent

Verified counts:

alice 3
bob 3
charlie 4
dave 1
eve 1
frank 1
grace 1
heidi 1
ivan 1
judy 1
mallory 3
oscar 1
peggy 2
trent 5
victor 1
walter 1

Expected output file created by the agent:

Path: /home/user/data/user_frequency.txt  
Permissions: 644 (rw-r--r--) owned by user

Exact contents (with a single trailing newline, no extra spaces):

trent 5
charlie 4
alice 3
bob 3
mallory 3
peggy 2
dave 1
eve 1
frank 1
grace 1
heidi 1
ivan 1
judy 1
oscar 1
victor 1
walter 1

The automated test will:

1. Confirm /home/user/data/user_frequency.txt exists.  
2. Verify its permissions (-rw-r--r--).  
3. Compare its exact content (including the final newline) against the block above.  
4. Ensure /home/user/data/users.log is still present and unchanged.
